Thursday, May 28, 2026 Daily Digest

GPTBots.ai Bridges Chat-to-Execution Gap, Skan AI's ABCF Framework, IAB Tackles AI Bot Problem

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

GPTBots.ai: From Chat to Execution

Aurora Mobile's enterprise AI platform has completed a major upgrade centered on knowledge base reconstruction, workflow execution, and enterprise governance. The key insight: Gartner predicts 40%+ of agentic AI projects will be canceled by 2027 — not because models aren't capable, but because AI can't plug into business systems. GPTBots is betting on knowledge graphs and hybrid retrieval to close the pilot-to-production gap.

Read on GlobeNewswire →
2

Skan AI Introduces ABCF Framework for Enterprise AI Agents

The Agentic Business Context Foundation (ABCF) captures what traditional enterprise systems miss: human reasoning, exceptions, and workarounds. "A 1% gap in observational coverage compounds to roughly a 40% failure rate by execution." This signals the operational intelligence layer your AI agents need to actually work in production.

Read on MarTech Series →
3

IAB Tech Lab Tackles the Growing AI Bot Problem

New guidance released today helps publishers and content owners decide who gets access to their content as AI crawlers proliferate. The framework complements the recently released CoMP API V1. Blanket bot blocking is no longer practical — the question is how to manage AI access strategically.

Read on MarTech →
4

Salesforce Connections 2026: The Agentic Marketing Moment

June 3-4 in Chicago. The main keynote promises "next-gen innovations to build toward becoming an agentic enterprise." Thursday's Agentforce Marketing Keynote focuses on how AI agents and marketers work together. If you're tracking where enterprise marketing is headed, this is the blueprint event.

Read on Salesforce →
5

Salesforce's Agentforce Reality Check

Mercury News examines the gap between AI promise and reality at Salesforce. The argument: if agentic AI genuinely automates enterprise sales, service, and marketing operations, whoever owns the CRM data captures disproportionate value. But deployments remain early, and revenue impact is still materializing.

Read on Mercury News →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The GPTBots.ai piece on why 40% of agentic AI projects fail. It's not about model capability anymore — it's about integration. Agents that can talk but can't execute into your actual business systems are expensive demos. The knowledge graph + workflow execution approach signals where enterprise AI is heading: context-aware agents that actually do things.
